title: rubus subcaesius
intro: Rubus subcaesius, also known as the dwarf raspberry or thimbleberry, is a type of deciduous shrub that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The leaves and roots of this plant are used for medicinal purposes, and it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The health benefits of Rubus subcaesius include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Rubus subcaesius can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is often used in herbal medicine to treat a range of conditions, from skin irritations to respiratory issues.
